column15: Avg. Not Predicated Off Threads Per Warp

column20: The optional metric smsp__pcsamp_sample_count could not be found. Collecting it as an additional metric could enable the rule to provide more guidance.

On average, each warp of this kernel spends 104.9 cycles being stalled waiting for a scoreboard dependency on a L1TEX (local, global, surface, texture) operation. Find the instruction producing the data being waited upon to identify the culprit. To reduce the number of cycles waiting on L1TEX data accesses verify the memory access patterns are optimal for the target architecture, attempt to increase cache hit rates by increasing data locality (coalescing), or by changing the cache configuration. Consider moving frequently used data to shared memory. This stall type represents about 90.4% of the total average of 116.0 cycles between issuing two instructions.

Check the Warp Stall Sampling (All Samples) table for the top stall locations in your source based on sampling data. The Kernel Profiling Guide (https://docs.nvidia.com/nsight-compute/ProfilingGuide/index.html#metrics-reference) provides more details on each stall reason.
